stem: In right triangle ABC with the right angle at C, point D is the foot of the altitude from C to hypotenuse AB. If angle A = 30 degrees and CD = 5\sqrt{3}, what is the area of triangle ABC?
explanation: First, find the sides of triangle ABC using the two sub-triangles created by the altitude CD. In right triangle ACD: angle A = 30 degrees and angle ADC = 90 degrees, so angle ACD = 60 degrees. Then AC = CD / sin(30) = 5sqrt(3) / (1/2) = 10sqrt(3), and AD = CD / tan(30) = 5sqrt(3) * sqrt(3) = 15. In right triangle BCD: angle B = 60 degrees and angle BDC = 90 degrees, so angle BCD = 30 degrees. Then BC = CD / sin(60) = 5sqrt(3) / (sqrt(3)/2) = 10, and BD = CD / tan(60) = 5sqrt(3) / sqrt(3) = 5. The hypotenuse AB = AD + BD = 15 + 5 = 20. The area of triangle ABC = (1/2) * AC * BC = (1/2)(10sqrt(3))(10) = 50sqrt(3). Verification: area = (1/2) * AB * CD = (1/2)(20)(5sqrt(3)) = 50sqrt(3).

distractorRationale.A: Computes area using BC and CD as if they were the two legs of the original triangle: (1/2)(10)(5sqrt(3)) = 25sqrt(3), but CD is the altitude to the hypotenuse, not a leg of triangle ABC = 25sqrt(3)
distractorRationale.B: Computes area using AC and CD instead of the actual legs AC and BC: (1/2)(10sqrt(3))(5sqrt(3)) = (1/2)(150) = 75, confusing the altitude CD with the leg BC = 75
distractorRationale.C: Correct: Finds AC = 10sqrt(3) and BC = 10 through the sub-triangles, then area = (1/2)(10sqrt(3))(10) = 50sqrt(3)
distractorRationale.D: Computes the product AC * BC = (10sqrt(3))(10) = 100sqrt(3) but omits the factor of 1/2 in the area formula = 100sqrt(3)

imageDescription: Right triangle ABC with the right angle marked at C. Vertex A is at the bottom-left, B at the bottom-right, and C at the top. The angle at A is labeled 30 degrees. Point D is on hypotenuse AB, with a perpendicular segment CD drawn from C to AB, marked with a right angle at D. The altitude CD is labeled 5sqrt(3). Segments AD and BD are indicated along the hypotenuse.
